source_pr.title: stmtsummary: fix unstable test
source_pr.body:
Signed-off-by: crazycs520 <crazycs520@gmail.com>

<!--

Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?
<!--

Please create an issue first to describe the problem.

There MUST be one line starting with "Issue Number:  " and 
linking the relevant issues via the "close" or "ref".

For more info, check https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/contribute-code.html#referring-to-an-issue.

-->

Issue Number: close #32046

### Check List

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->


- [ ] No code

### Release note

<!-- bugfix or new feature needs a release note -->

```release-note
None
```

root_cause_explanation: The statement-summary test exercised concurrent operations (adding statements while concurrently shrinking `MaxStmtCount`) and implicitly relied on a particular scheduling/ordering for eviction to happen before assertions. Under different goroutine interleavings, the expected eviction/extra record could fail to materialize in time, making the test unstable. This PR stabilizes the test by restructuring the concurrency (using a WaitGroup wrapper) and driving additional statement inserts after `SetMaxStmtCount` so eviction deterministically occurs before checking the summary rows.
fix_pattern: When testing concurrent code paths, avoid assertions that depend on goroutine scheduling. Add explicit synchronization and/or trigger the expected state transition (e.g., force eviction after config changes) before asserting on results.
